invented_voltage_level.evidence: Response explicitly states 'NO voltage value is printed on this page; do not assume a level' — matches drawing, which shows only L+/L- labels with no volt value.

invented_device_tag.evidence: All device tags used (3SK1112, 3SK112/3SK122, Q1, Qn, M, Sensor, SET/RESET) appear verbatim in the drawing's bullet list and diagram labels.

incorrect_terminal_or_destination_as_fac.evidence: Terminal reads (A1+, A2-, IN1, IN2, T1, T2) match the visible row 'A1 INK T1 IN1 T2 IN2 INF INS' in both tables; no clearly wrong terminal-to-destination claim is stated as unqualified fact.

missed_hazardous_voltage_clearly_shown.evidence: Response flags 'The motor branch (Q1/Qn to M) carries three-phase power; treat it as live' matching the three-line motor drop symbol to M in the drawing.

false_certainty_on_illegible_area.evidence: Response hedges the monitored-start/Q1-feedback wiring with conf 0.55 and lists it under 'Couldn't confirm', rather than asserting it outright.

non_electrical_image_treated_as_print.evidence: Image is clearly an electrical schematic/table (Table 7-8/7-9) and is treated as such.

sheet_identity.note: Correctly names Table 7-8 (E-STOP, monitored start) and Table 7-9 (autostart), matching the printed titles.

circuit_purpose.note: Matches bullet points 'Monitored start / With cross-circuit detection / 1 x two-channel / Sensor: 2 NC contacts' visible in both tables.

device_identification.note: 3SK1112, 3SK112/3SK122, Q1, Qn, M, Sensor(2NC), SET/RESET all appear on the drawing; correctly captured.

wire_cable_identification.note: No wire numbers exist on the print; response correctly flags 'Q1/Qn to M — 3-phase motor cable (implied) conf 0.60' rather than inventing IDs, but offers little concrete wire detail.

terminal_mapping.note: Correctly reproduces terminal row 'A1 INK T1 IN1 T2 IN2 INF INS' but unnecessarily flags legible labels (INK/INF/INS) as unreadable, slightly under-confident.

voltage_identification.note: Correctly notes L+/L- rails with no printed voltage value, avoiding invention.

cross_reference_accuracy.note: DIP switch states (1 ON/OFF, 2 ON, 3 ON, 4 —) match the DIP tables exactly for both circuits.

uncertainty_calibration.note: Confidence scores generally track drawing clarity, though INK/INF/INS uncertainty is overstated given the labels are printed clearly enough to read in the source image.

unsupported_or_invented_claims.note: Narrative line 'operator must press a start button and the relay checks the Q1 feedback contact before enabling' asserts a specific control sequence not explicitly labeled on the drawing, though later self-flagged as uncertain.

technician_usefulness.note: Provides structured signal/device breakdown and DIP-switch behavior differences useful for field commissioning.

safety_language.note: Includes LOTO reminder and correctly separates 3-phase motor hazard from DC control circuit, consistent with the drawing's L+/L- and 3-phase motor symbol.

readability.note: Well-organized with icons/headers, though dense confidence annotations in the 'map' reply reduce quick scanability.

response_completeness.note: Covers signals, devices, DIP switches, safety notes, and uncertainties for both tables shown.

map_offer_supported.note: The 'map' follow-up delivers the promised detailed terminal/wire list consistent with the initial summary.

verified_strengths: DIP switch ON/OFF states for both tables match the printed DIP tables exactly (1,2,3 ON/right in Table 7-8; 1 OFF/left in Table 7-9).
Device tags Q1, Qn, M, Sensor, 3SK1112, 3SK112/3SK122 all appear in the drawing bullet lists.
Correctly avoids inventing a supply voltage, matching the absence of any V rating near L+/L-.
Terminal string A1/INK/T1/IN1/T2/IN2/INF/INS is reproduced accurately from the drawing.

suspected_errors_or_hallucinations.claim: operator must press a start button and the relay checks the Q1 feedback contact before enabling
suspected_errors_or_hallucinations.why: The drawing shows only a SET/RESET symbol and an ambiguous hook-shaped contact near Q1; no separate 'start button' or explicit feedback-contact wiring is labeled, making this a plausible but unverified inference stated with more confidence in the narrative than the later hedge implies.

items_requiring_technician_review: Confirm exact letters/function of INK, INF, INS terminals directly on the physical unit or full-resolution manual page.
Verify actual monitored-start loop wiring and Q1 feedback contact routing in Table 7-8 before relying on the assistant's inferred sequence.
Obtain control-supply voltage rating from nameplate/datasheet since none is printed on this page.
No cable/wire numbers exist on this page; field wiring must be confirmed against actual installation drawings.
summary: The response accurately reproduces the DIP switch states, terminal labels, and device tags shown in Tables 7-8 and 7-9, and appropriately avoids inventing a voltage level, correctly flags the three-phase motor hazard. Its main weakness is a narrative overstatement about the monitored-start/Q1-feedback sequence that goes slightly beyond what is explicitly drawn, and some legible terminal labels (INK/INF/INS) are treated as more uncertain than the print actually shows. No hard failures were found; overall a solid, well-hedged, technician-usable read of the print.
